## [00:00 - 03:02]

Let's hop right into the foundations of online business. Products, services, and traffic. So you need a product or service and targeted traffic. That's just how things work. I've heard this question a few times and Twitter isn't like YouTube. You just can't monetize tweet views. That's not how it works. Twitter should be treated as a traffic source to your offer. As with any other social media business. So Twitter makes it easier to do that because you can post links. You can post links and threads. People can retweet your tweets so it gets massive exposure. You can put your links in your bio in your location area. It's just a very simple way compared to other social media channels to have people click on your offers and eventually buy them. An ebook, a membership, a course is a product or physical product. Anything you can imagine that's not a service, right? A service is something like web design, copywriting, a mentorship service, coaching calls. Other high income skills can be considered a service and yes, they can be sold on Twitter. You can affiliate for products and services, which is very popular, which is pretty much you're taking a product or service that is already doing very well and you are helping them. You are helping them make more money and you get a commission of that. So if you're not necessarily an expert in any given topic, you can affiliate for people that are experts in topics that you talk about or that your audience would want to learn more about. And as we talked about Twitter or social media in general is a traffic source. The more niche down your audience is, the better you can promote your products or services. We talked about this a bit when we were optimizing your profile and all of that stuff. If you built a brand solely based on web design or freelancing as a web designer, you're going to have a hyper targeted audience, right? It may take longer to grow, but imagine selling a web design product through your page. It would sell like crazy, but you don't have to niche yourself down that far in order to sell a product that your audience wants. So trading Twitter as what it is a traffic source. You don't need to sell directly from Twitter. You don't need to send people directly to a gum road landing page or it would be kind of silly to send people to a landing page to sell a high ticket service. That's just not how it works. You need to direct your audience to the right information. You can drive traffic to a blog, for example, you can build your email list. You can post a YouTube video.

## [03:02 - 04:23]

Just think of it as a part of a funnel. You're providing value and you can promote your products or services in the place that is the best fit. So for online business, you need a basic understanding of marketing in general, copywriting for product or service landing pages, email marketing, web design for blogs, such landing pages and offer creation. So once you understand marketing in general, this whole thing will start to make a bit more sense because as we talked about, that's all it is. That's all Twitter or making money on Twitter is. It is starting a business and using Twitter as a traffic source to your offer or free content. Also, don't get overwhelmed by these because as you're building your audience, you can learn the basics of each skill one at a time. You can learn as you grow. You can use that as content for your own Twitter. And then by the time that you're ready to monetize, you can do it very well. And with that, I just wanted to give you a basic introduction of online business in general. Next, we will talk about creating your offer and getting you ready to being that much closer to making your first dollar online.
